column1: C[Mef: O8 feels early to be this high (normally opening moves hang around 3rd or 4th lines unless they are special purpose)

Mef: Also black should look to avoid playing empty triangles unless absolute necessary.  S14 is an empty triangle (and an inefficient shape), likewise N16 felt slow (and was also an empty triangle). E4, E5, F10, P3 and N8 can be put on this same list.  Black would see huge gains just from checking for empty triangles before making his move, and if it does make an empty triangle, try to find a better move that does the same thing (=
C[matburt: I think this game is pretty much over, thanks for schooling me :)

C[Mef: Ignoring R3 is normally not suggested (since it is basically a contact move), but I think the real problem black wants to look at is staying connected with his N4 /Q4 stones.  O3 basically forces white to play P4 (which cuts black in two).
Mef: Instead black may want to play P4 or O4 - keep all his stones as once strong group
Mef: C13 is an empty triangle, an inefficient shape.   Black's group would be stronger if this was at B13 (a more efficient shape) also H12 is big because it keeps white split apart.

Playing a stone with only two liberties like this means white gets a choice of forcing moves (basically black's stone was weaker than white's stones the moment it was played, and itwhite
